relatedness_reasoning: Both files share customer identity concepts and risk/compliance assessment data; risk_and_compliance consumes credit_score_result and produces kyc_refresh_request, forming a logical pipeline where credit assessment triggers KYC refresh workflows.

mappings.reasoning: No equivalent field in inbound; system-generated unique identifier for the outbound request.
Inbound timestamp represents when credit assessment was generated; outbound timestamp likely derived from or set around the same time the KYC refresh is triggered.
Inbound destination_department is 'risk_and_compliance', which becomes the source department for the outbound KYC refresh request.
Inbound source_department is 'credit', which becomes the destination for the outbound KYC refresh request sent back to credit.
Priority may be derived from risk level; high risk assessments could trigger HIGH priority KYC refresh requests.
Exact field match; customer identifier carries through unchanged.
Inbound 'name' field maps to outbound 'full_name'; same customer name concept with different field naming.
Exact field match; date of birth carries through unchanged.
No national ID or passport field exists in inbound schema; likely sourced from external KYC system or customer master data.
No email field exists in inbound credit score result; likely sourced from customer master data or contact system.
No phone field exists in inbound credit score result; likely sourced from customer master data or contact system.
No prior KYC refresh date exists in inbound; likely sourced from KYC history or customer master data.
Refresh may be triggered by periodic credit assessment; weak connection as inbound does not explicitly state refresh reason.
Risk flag is derived from fraud risk assessment or default risk level; boolean flag set if either indicates elevated risk.
Inbound default_risk_level ('Low') maps to outbound risk_level; same risk assessment concept with different enum values.
Compliance flags from inbound may populate trigger factors in outbound; both represent risk or compliance concerns.
Compliance issues array is constructed from inbound compliance flags and regulatory concerns with added status and severity fields.
No document requirements exist in inbound credit score result; determined by KYC policy or risk assessment rules.
No required actions exist in inbound; determined by KYC policy or risk assessment rules.
No deadline exists in inbound; calculated based on KYC policy and current timestamp.
Inbound review_status ('Completed') may trigger outbound status ('PENDING_CUSTOMER_RESPONSE'); weak connection as values differ significantly.
Inbound recommended_action may inform internal notes; weak connection as inbound field is structured recommendation, not free-form notes.
